channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Fake the interview: self-taught path to remote roles
item.description: Pass remote tech interviews by mastering system design stories, not LeetCode drills — 3 real designs, 30-minute prep, and the exact commands to mock a distributed system locally in 2026.

item.title: Vector DBs: Waste of money?
item.description: Cut vector DB costs 70% with pgvector 0.7.0 — benchmarked insert rates, p99 latency, and $216/year vs $688/year in 2026.

item.title: From $500 to $8k/month: the 4 moves that worked
item.description: From $500 to $8k/month in 4 moves: how one developer flipped the script on global rates, saved 20 % in fees, and cut invoice time from 6 weeks to 3 days.

item.title: Fixed burnout in 8 weeks: freelance dev recovery
item.description: Recover from freelance burnout in 8 weeks with real systems—financial buffer, scope protection, and recovery blocks. Track your true hourly rate, bug rate, and client satisfaction with version-pinned tools like Linear 2026.3 and Toggl Track 2026.1.

item.title: Skip the star projects: show work not words
item.description: Portfolio gets you hired remotely from Africa — ship one endpoint, expose one metric, land the interview. Use FastAPI 0.111, PostgreSQL 16, AWS RDS t4g.micro. Live demo beats polished README by 78% callbacks.

item.title: Edge functions in 2026: when they actually help
item.description: pped from 60 ms to 14 ms, but the dashboard s with Edge functions them waste — solve latency by running code closer to users.

item.title: Queues work until they don't
item.description: e hitting 100% CPU, queues backing up, and j with Queues until they don — this when I inherited a system that used RabbitMQ 3.

item.title: Big tech exodus: what they won’t tell you
item.description: Cut mean API response time 40 % with Prometheus SLOs — learn what senior engineers at Google, Meta, and Amazon say they really left for, and how to measure velocity decay before it kills your team.

item.title: Zero-trust for 5-person teams: skip the noise
item.description: Reduce attack surface 70% with zero-trust for small teams — MFA, short-lived tokens, and 3 concrete steps to start today.

item.title: Pick the right live update tech in 2026
item.description: Choose WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or long polling in 2026 with real benchmarks and cost data — avoid the 1.2 second lag I debugged in production.

item.title: 60k pay gap: prompt ops vs vector tuning
item.description: In 2026, prompt engineering with guardrails pays $152k median, vector search tuning pays $178k—combined skills hit $210k. See the benchmark results and decide which skill to learn first.

item.title: Local LLMs on a laptop: the real stack
item.description: Cut local LLM latency from 12 s to 1.8 s with the stack that actually works in 2026 — Rust tokenizers, vLLM, and pre-warmed inference. Real benchmarks and code.

item.title: Trace LLM pipelines end-to-end with OpenTelemetry
item.description: Cut LLM latency debugging time 60% with OpenTelemetry 1.41 traces and Prometheus metrics — prompts, tokens, GPU wait times and the cache stampede mistake most teams miss.

item.title: AI learns your codebase: why 80% of repos stay
item.description: Cut codebase context confusion 80% with AI that learns your entire repo — static, runtime, and behavioral layers with 2026 tools, benchmarks, and real fixes. Repository intelligence explained for engineers.

item.title: Skip zero-trust overkill: small team tactics
item.description: Save resources with practical zero-trust security for small teams. Cut $12,000/year by focusing on simple IAM roles, MFA, and secrets rotation.

item.title: AI salary boosters: transformers vs vector DBs in 2026
item.description: In 2026, fine-tuning transformers pays 9–13 % more than running vector DBs, but only if the AI is the product. See latency, cost, and hiring data for both stacks.

item.title: Senior without CS: How I shipped my way up
item.description: Senior engineer without a CS degree: measure before optimizing, own the stack, and learn from outages. Latency drops from 2.8s to 320ms, losses cut from $18k to $800, and cloud bills normalized with Terraform 1.6 and Prometheus 2.47.

item.title: MCP servers demystified for devs
item.description: M) with a 5% CPU utilization. - Cost: Runn with MCP servers you — implement a microservice architecture, you've likel.

item.title: Tune your pool size: the one database setting you're
item.description: Cut database wait time 60% by right-sizing your pool — the textbook formula is wrong in 2026, and here is the real math with concrete benchmarks and tools.

item.title: Rebuilt my freelance grind
item.description: Cut freelance burnout 80% with 3 concrete fixes — hourly rate up 25%, error rate down 72%, and mood score up 56%. Learn what tools worked, what costs I cut, and the exact scripts I used.
